title: Level 59

{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 59: "The Sins of a Massive Mall"}} {{Headerbox|header=In the event that you are planning on utilizing a personal check to cover the expenses accrued while shopping, we strongly encourage you to seek out the help of a service desk attendant while you are utilizing any of our available cashiers." It is strongly recommended that you ensure that your check, though personal, has been cleared in order to avoid any potential issues with payment at a later date. - Speakers|file=Level307better.jpg}} ==Description== [[File:Level307better.jpg|center|700px]]
'''Level 59''' known as “The Sins of a Massive Mall” takes the form of an expansive shopping area, featuring various retail stores that would be normally found in a nall. These stores are found scattered throughout the area and range in size. It is noted that the lights within this environment are not functional and instead flicker at a erratic and spontaneous pace. The reason for the lights erratic, flickering behaviour cannot be accurately determined at this point. The rooms in the majority of the environment appear to have been constructed of both painted and unpainted concrete. The architecture of this environment mainly consists of square and rectangular shapes, which can be seen in both the walls and floors. All of the rooms are mostly empty, though they contain a varying amount of supplies ranging from empty bottles of [[Object 1|Almond Water]], to other supplies such as a few empty battery packets. The contents of the stores are often not of notable brands, and can instead commonly be a variety of unknown and in some cases, dangerous objects. Wanderers traversing the landscape of '''Level 59''' have often reported the feeling that there is something ominous about the environment. Reports of the air and atmosphere feeling heavy or even claustrophobic, as if some force weighing down are common, along with accounts of a feeling a creeping, paranoid feeling when traveling through the rooms and hallways of this environment. All of these senses and feelings though not inherently dangerous, ma cause discomfort and paranoia to anyone unfortunate enough to find themselves traveling through this environment. [[File:Amallroom.jpg|center|600x600px|link=]]
Upon entering the environment, a faint distinct sound will become audible. This sound is that of distant but constant music. This music, though at a low volume, may still be clearly heard by wanderers and adds to the already ominous feel that this environment exhibits. This sound has been characterized by many different explorers and wanderers alike, describing the music being nostalgic, yet having an ominous tone and a noticeable difference from its normal real world counterpart. During the time spent in '''Level 59''', one may hear announcements of one variety or another. While these announcements are mostly innocuous, sometimes the announcements or service desk notices are read out by an almost mechanical voice. This voice is often described and lacking any sort of tone or human elements, leaving the listener with an uncanny and chilling sensation. In '''Level 59''', there are elevators and stairs which lead to the higher floors and lower floors below. There is currently no confirmed number of how many floors this environment consists of, but there is believed to be a finite though expansive amount of floors that can be travelled through. The existence of these floors can be confirmed by the presence and use of elevators as well as the design and size of the level itself. ==Entities== '''Level 59''' is not known or suspected to have any entities of any kind. This is in part due to the lack of any sightings of any entities at this level, as well as the lack of any noticeable evidence for the presence of any entity having been within or around this level at any point. ==Colonies and Outposts== Due to the nature of the level, colonies and outposts cannot be established in the level. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrance=== * Wandering far enough in [[Level 33]] will lead here; * Finding a ladder in [[Level 34]], and climbing the ladder will lead here; * Finding a rare emergency exit door in [[Level 58]] and entering it will lead to '''Level 59'''; * Lastly, Noclipping in a table at [[Level 521]] will lead here. === Exits === * Opening a computer and executing Level_60.exe will lead to [[Level 60]]; * Finding a rare exit door will lead to [[Level 61]]; * Lastly, finding a staircase that leads underground will lead to [[Level 948]]. ---- {{Redirect|Prev=58|Next=60}}
